context.profile.md:
Name: Jane Smith
Work Experience:
- Senior Analyst at DataCo (Jan 2020 – Present): ETL pipelines, 40% query improvement
- Analyst at StartupX (Mar 2018 – Dec 2019): BI dashboards
Projects:
- OpenPipeline: Open-source ETL framework, Python, 500 GitHub stars
Skills: Python, SQL, Spark, data modeling
context.narrative.md:
Positioning: Data engineer bridging analytics and engineering
Target Roles: Senior Data Engineer
What I'm looking for: High-growth B2B SaaS companies building data infrastructure

tests.expected_output: personal-site-draft.md created with all 6 page sections: Home/Hero, About, Work/Experience, Projects, Skills, Contact. Each section is independently copyable.
assertions: Home/Hero section present with headline (8 words max), subheadline (20 words max), and short bio (50 words)
About page has long bio (150–200 words), By the numbers block, and What I'm looking for
Work/Experience page covers each role with narrative description
Projects page includes OpenPipeline with stack and outcome
Skills page groups skills with one per line (not comma-separated)
Contact page has friendly invitation and what user is open to
User told where file was saved
Skill first asks about existing platform before generating

context.profile.md:
Name: Alex Rivera
Work Experience:
- Software Engineer at MegaCo (Jan 2021 – Present): Microservices, 10M req/day
Skills: Go, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, distributed systems
context.narrative.md:
Positioning: Backend engineer building for scale
Target: Startup engineering roles

tests.expected_output: personal-site-draft.md created with copy formatted with Framer in mind (component-friendly sections, clear copy blocks). All 6 pages present.
assertions: Skill asked about platform and user responded Framer
Copy formatted to be drop-in friendly for Framer component structure

context.profile.md:
Name: Michael Chen
Work Experience:
- CTO at ScaleCo (Jan 2018 – Present): 80-person eng org, $50M ARR
- VP Engineering at GrowthCo (Jan 2015 – Dec 2017): 40 engineers
Skills: engineering leadership, cloud architecture, technical strategy
context.narrative.md:
Positioning: Engineering executive who scales organizations
What I'm looking for: CTO roles at Series C+ companies

tests.expected_output: Personal site copy emphasizes leadership and org scale rather than individual technical contributions. By the numbers block surfaces team sizes and ARR, not code metrics.
assertions: Hero headline emphasizes leadership impact, not technical skills
By the numbers includes '80-person engineering org' or '$50M ARR'
Work page descriptions are narrative (not bullet-list style)
Contact page What I'm looking for references CTO and Series C+
